fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

course_description.character: Two distinct races out of Lienz, East Tyrol: the 112 km Classic Dolomitenradrundfahrt (~1,870m) and the 218 km SuperGiroDolomiti (~5,060m) that this profile's vitals describe. The SuperGiro crosses the Austria-Italy border into Carinthia and Friuli, taking in Monte Zoncolan alongside the passes shared with the Classic route, before returning to Lienz. The Dolomitenradrundfahrt itself is Austria's oldest cycling marathon, first held in 1951; the SuperGiro is a later addition, launched in 2014 as the harder long-distance option (the 2026 editions were the 38th Dolomitenradrundfahrt and 11th SuperGiroDolomiti).
course_description.signature_challenge: Monte Zoncolan — one of cycling's hardest climbs, tackled only on the 218 km SuperGiroDolomiti alongside a double ascent of the Plöckenpass.
suffering_zones: Gailbergsattel — used by both the Classic and SuperGiro routes
Plöckenpass — climbed twice on the SuperGiro, the border crossing into Italy
Monte Zoncolan — the defining climb of the long route
Kartitscher Sattel through the Lesachtal — a late, remote test as the route returns toward Lienz

logistics.lodging_strategy: Lienz offers hotels and guesthouses; book early for event weekend.

biased_opinion.summary: First run in 1951, the Dolomitenradrundfahrt is a genuine Austrian institution — 38 editions deep by 2026 and billed as the country's oldest cycling marathon. The 218 km SuperGiroDolomiti, added in 2014, is the race this profile's numbers describe: a border-crossing route into Italy that tackles Monte Zoncolan, one of cycling's hardest climbs, alongside a double Plöckenpass ascent. More than 2,000 riders from 26 countries took part across both distances in 2026. The organizer's own material has minor inconsistencies (a one-year gap in founding-year claims, some pages still citing the SuperGiro's older 228-240 km course length), which is worth knowing but doesn't undercut the core event's substance.
strengths: Genuine, long-running institution — first held in 1951, one of Austria's oldest cycling marathons
Monte Zoncolan on the SuperGiro route — a legitimately brutal, world-class climb, not just a marketing name
Cross-border route through Austria, Carinthia, and Friuli adds real geographic and cultural variety
Two distance tiers (112 km Classic, 218 km SuperGiro) let riders choose their day
weaknesses: Course-length data drift: some secondary and tourism pages still cite an older 228-240 km SuperGiro distance instead of the current 218 km
The organizer's own material is inconsistent about the founding year (1951 vs. a 'since 1950' figure on other pages)
Firm cutoffs — 19:30 for the SuperGiro, 16:30 for the Classic — with no recorded finish for riders arriving later
€125-145 entry for the SuperGiro is a meaningful cost for a race without a marquee international brand name
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A genuinely historic race with a genuinely hard signature climb in Zoncolan — go for the SuperGiro if you want the full test, the Classic if 112 km and 1,870m is enough. Just don't trust every course-length figure you find outside the organizer's current official pages.

final_verdict.one_liner: Legendary Austrian road ultra with epic Dolomites climbing and 70+ years of history.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a genuinely historic Alpine marathon with a world-class signature climb (Zoncolan) and don't mind firm time cutoffs. Consider the shorter Classic route if 218 km/5,060m is more than you want to take on.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want Zoncolan without the full SuperGiro distance: research standalone Zoncolan climbing routes from Ovaro. If you want a similarly historic Austrian/Italian border-region gran fondo: Alpenbrevet (Switzerland) covers comparable Alpine-pass volume.

videos.title: Dolomitenradrundfahrt 2011 - Lienz
videos.description: Am Pfingstsonntag ging die 24. Auflage der Dolomitenradrundfahrt in Lienz über die Bühne. Obwohl der Regen das Teilnehmerfeld schrumpfen ließ, nahmen rund 1000 Radler die Tour von Lienz über Kötschach-Mauthen, Maria Luggau, Kartitsch und Abfaltersbach in Angriff.

videos.title: Highlights Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel 2025
videos.description:
Lüm. Light. 
The light of the rising sun, the light between the peaks, the light in the eyes of every rider. 
7964 hearts ignited, three legendary routes, one shared emotion.
We celebrated the light, outside and within. 

Every pedal stroke was a spark. Every finish line, a burst of pure energy.
The winners were those who gave their all. 
The real victory was lighting the road with heart.
This was the 38th Maratona dles Dolomites.
And from today, that light will never fade.

In 2026, we return, guided by a new word: Pesc. Peace.
Fragile. Powerful. Essential.
Let it lead us, toward kindness, toward calm, toward a better world.

Giulan de cör. 🌿
See you on July 5th, 2026.

videos.title: Slight chaos at Maratona dles Dolomites 2025
videos.description:
The Maratona dles Dolomites (Ladin for "Dolomites Marathon"; Italian: Maratona delle Dolomiti), is an annual single-day road bicycle race covering seven mountain passes in the Dolomites. Open to amateur cyclists, the Maratona—with 9,000 riders chosen from 30,000 applicants,[1] from over 70 nations—is one of the biggest Italian Granfondo bicycle races.[2][3] National Geographic described it as "one of the biggest, most passionate, and most chaotic bike races on Earth."[4]

Date First week in July
Region Trentino-South Tyrol, Italy
Nickname(s) Maratona dles Dolomites
Discipline Road
Type Granfondo: Single-day race
Organiser Maratona dles Dolomites Committee
Race director Michil Costa

rider_intel.search_text: The provided transcript from the Dolomitenradrundfahrt / SuperGiroDolomiti video does not contain race-specific content or rider commentary about the event. The transcript appears to consist primarily of song lyrics and music rather than actionable intelligence about this 135-mile gravel race through East Tyrol, Austria. No information about course conditions, terrain characteristics, key challenges, gear recommendations, or race strategy was found in the available material. Additional video content with actual race commentary would be needed to provide meaningful rider intelligence for this challenging Alpine gravel event.
